concept_ref: §3 row 5 — 'One test per condition is enough for MCDC'

scenario.title: Simple Safety Gate
scenario.narrative: A safety system activates when sensor A is triggered AND button B is pressed.

claim: This 3-test set satisfies MCDC for both conditions.
hints: Re-read Law Card 6. An independence pair fixes every condition except one and the outcome must flip.
For condition A: which two tests differ ONLY in A and produce different outcomes? Same question for B.
Solution — A's pair: T1 vs T2 (only A flips, outcome flips). B's pair: T1 vs T3 (only B flips, outcome flips). Three tests are enough here because T1 is shared.

seeded_faults.description: Button B check is bypassed when sensor A is triggered

misconceptions.explanation_md: You tested each condition in isolation. MCDC requires *paired* test cases where one condition changes while all others remain fixed and the decision outcome flips.
